threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of the 'local' and find yourself confused by the endless vegan options.
Caught a glimpse of a guy holding a full fried chicken box on the 59 last night. He was negotiating the perfect dip into BBQ sauce while the bus swayed. Pure Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids setting up a makeshift café in the park, complete with hand-drawn menus. That’s the spirit of Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that turns into a bar after 10 PM, serving cocktails with döner meat on the side. It's wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they have a favorite taco spot that isn’t on Yelp and can argue about the best burrito on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that stopped for five minutes because a guy was playing sax on the sidewalk. Classic SF vibe—music over schedule.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's squares are just for eating and drinking, but locals know they're actually for catching up with friends and spontaneous music jams.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to try the 'croqueta de jamón' from a local bar—they still think only tapas count.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies by their puzzled looks at the bus timetables - like they expect them to run on time or something!
Caught the N133 last night, overheard a couple arguing about a broken mug. Ends up it was a wedding gift. The whole bus went silent just to listen.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a street performer juggling flaming torches while a kebab vendor argued with a customer about missing garlic sauce. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can spot spontaneous street performances at U-Bahn stations, turning a regular commute into an unexpected mini-festival.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell when someone’s from here by how they react to a BART delay. True locals roll their eyes, while newbies just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 with an opera singer belting out Puccini. The crowd was confused, but you could see the smiles. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell, but they miss the real vibe in our local squares, where the best street music happens for free.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them struggling with the 'tapa' concept, ordering tap water at bars like it's the norm. Classic rookie mistake!
